p: Here you will find all my favorite recipes, carefully curated for every occasion and mood. Let's start cooking!
Each recipe is tagged with badges to help you quickly find what fits your needs.
A full Christmas feast — herb-seared beef tenderloin, all the sides, and two dessert options.
½ tsp porcini mushroom powder (optional — adds deep umami)
Mix all dry rub ingredients together in a bowl.
Pat tenderloin dry and trim any silver skin.
Remove from fridge 60–90 minutes before cooking.
Rest 15 minutes, then slice and serve.
Roast at 425°F for 20–28 minutes until internal temp reaches 125–130°F.
Rub with olive oil, then coat evenly with the rub.
Sear on all sides in a hot cast iron pan.
½ cup DF cream cheese or cashew cream
Whip coconut cream and cream cheese together until smooth.
Fold in horseradish, Dijon, lemon juice, and seasoning.
Chill at least 1 hour before serving.
This sauce can be made up to 2 days in advance — it actually gets better as the flavors meld.
Add potatoes, water, and salt to the Instant Pot.
Pressure cook on HIGH for 12 minutes; quick release.
Drain, then mash with DF butter, milk, and roasted garlic.
Add beef drippings to a muffin tin and heat in oven until smoking hot.
Whisk flour, milk, eggs, and salt into a smooth batter.
Carefully pour batter into the hot tins, filling halfway.
Bake 20–25 minutes. Do not open the oven.
Opening the oven during baking will cause the Yorkshire puddings to deflate. Set a timer and resist.
2 lbs carrots, peeled and cut on the diagonal
Toss carrots with olive oil, maple syrup, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a baking sheet and roast 25–30 minutes until caramelized.
Toss sprouts with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Add bacon if using.
While roasting, reduce balsamic, pomegranate juice, maple syrup, and rosemary in a saucepan until syrupy.
Toss roasted sprouts in glaze and return to oven 3–4 minutes.
Toss apple slices with maple syrup, cinnamon, and lemon juice. Spread in a baking dish.
Mix oats, almond flour, brown sugar, and DF butter until crumbly. Spread over apples.
Bake 35–40 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Serve warm with dairy-free ice cream or coconut whipped cream.
Combine Merlot, sugar, cinnamon, star anise, cloves, and orange peel in a deep saucepan.
Bring to a simmer and stir until sugar dissolves.
Add pears and poach 20–25 minutes, turning occasionally, until tender.
Remove pears and reduce the poaching liquid to a syrup.
Serve pears drizzled with the warm syrup.
Pears can be poached 1–2 days ahead and stored in the syrup in the fridge. Gently reheat before serving.
A full Thanksgiving spread. All recipes are dairy-free, gluten-free, and soy-free. Includes a day-of cooking timeline for a 4:00 PM dinner.
½ cup apple juice, apple cider, or water
½ cup brown sugar or coconut sugar
¼ cup Dijon or whole-grain mustard (GF)
Combine all glaze ingredients in a small saucepan.
Foil for covering and lining the pan
Place ham cut-side down on the rack and cover loosely with foil.
Pour ½ cup apple juice or cider into the bottom of the pan.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Line roasting pan with foil and place rack inside.
Remove foil for the last 30 minutes and brush glaze every 10 minutes.
Simmer over medium heat ~5 minutes, stirring until slightly thickened. Set aside.
Tent with foil and rest 10–15 minutes before slicing.
3 lb Yukon Gold or Russet potatoes, peeled and cubed
½ cup oat milk or coconut milk
¼ cup olive oil or dairy-free butter
Add potatoes, water, and salt to the Instant Pot.
Seal lid and pressure cook on HIGH for 10 minutes.
Quick release pressure and drain potatoes.
Return to pot; let steam dissipate 1–2 minutes.
Mash with dairy-free butter and milk until smooth.
Season to taste and keep warm.
2 tbsp olive oil or dairy-free butter
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk in GF flour to form a roux; cook 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in broth until smooth.
Add coconut aminos, salt, and pepper.
3 lb yams or sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
¼ cup maple syrup or brown sugar
¼ cup coconut cream or oat milk
3 tbsp olive oil or dairy-free butter
½ cup chopped pecans or pumpkin seeds (optional)
Boil yams 15–20 minutes until tender; drain.
Mash with sweetener, coconut cream, DF butter, vanilla, spices, and salt.
Spread into a baking dish and top with marshmallows (and nuts if using).
Bake 15–20 minutes until marshmallows are golden.
2 lb Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved
Toss sprouts in olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Simmer balsamic vinegar and maple syrup 5 minutes to reduce.
Drizzle glaze over sprouts before serving.
Toss carrots with olive oil, herbs, salt, and pepper.

l: Crispy Parmesan–Panko Air Fryer Pork Chops
p: Boneless, ¼-inch thick pork chops with a crispy golden parmesan-panko crust. Fast, foolproof, and packed with flavor.
½ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese
Preheat air fryer to 400°F (205°C) for 3–5 minutes.
Dry the pork chops completely with paper towels.
Make binder — mix mayonnaise and Dijon mustard until smooth.
Make breading — combine all breading ingredients in a shallow dish and mix well.
Coat each chop with the mayo–Dijon binder, then press firmly into breading on both sides.
Spray the air fryer basket lightly with olive oil. Arrange chops in a single layer and mist the tops.
Air fry 4 minutes. Flip carefully, mist again, and cook 3–4 more minutes until golden brown.
Check temp — pork is done at 145°F internal temperature. Rest 2 minutes before serving.
Use a light mist of oil only — oversaturating causes steaming instead of crisping
If oil smokes or browns too fast, reduce temperature to 390°F
